{"step_by_step_thinking": "The periodontal ligament (PDL) fibers connect the cementum of the tooth to the alveolar bone. When a tooth tips, the fibers on the side where the tooth moves are stretched as the tooth is pulled away from the bone, while the fibers on the opposite side are compressed as the tooth is pushed into the bone. This results in a scenario where the PDL fibers are both compressed and stretched, not all compressed or all stretched.", "answer_choice": "C"}
